agency: Office of the Staten Island Borough President (BPSI)
method: Outside-in crawl (browser UA, robots-respecting — statenislandusa.com/robots.txt disallows only /ajax/, /apps/, /ida.html, /stayonstatenisland.html). Fingerprinted www.statenislandusa.com as a Weebly brochure site behind Cloudflare (server: cloudflare, cf-ray; x-host: *.weebly.net; weebly-anchor.js; Weebly Forms posting to weebly.com/apps/formSubmit.php; Constant Contact newsletter). Walked the sitemap and key pages (cbapplication, bpassist, budget, borough-board, community-events, notices, staffdirectory). Verified the NYC Open Data agency label 'Staten Island Borough President (BPSI)' via the Socrata Discovery API (display attribution is 'Office of the Staten Island Borough President') and pulled all 2 assets with column schemas.

meta.platform: Weebly brochure site behind Cloudflare (Weebly Forms + Constant Contact); no CMS of record, no API
meta.netnew: CommunityBoardApplication — apply to serve on a Staten Island community board (today an opaque Weebly form)
meta.tagline: The thinnest domain yet: a Weebly brochure site, two trivial datasets, and none of the borough president's charter work available as data.
meta.headline: A borough president office with essentially no data or API — and four identical sibling offices — is not a replatform, it is a case for one shared, federated Borough President API.

scorecard_notes.platform: Weebly DIY site builder behind Cloudflare — no CMS of record or application server
scorecard_notes.siteApi: No site API of any kind; no JSON, no OpenAPI
scorecard_notes.openData: Two BPSI datasets: one aggregate (3fes-huds, ends FY20), one zero-column blob (mmut-uup9)
scorecard_notes.coreData: Land use, appointments, budget, resolutions — none of the charter work exists as data
scorecard_notes.writeApi: BP Assist and the community board application are web/Weebly forms; no API
scorecard_notes.agent: Nothing agent-native; no MCP, no structured contract
scorecard_notes.ownership: Owns almost nothing — site on Weebly, forms on Weebly, email on Constant Contact, data on Socrata

headline_findings: The Office of the Staten Island Borough President has the thinnest technical footprint assessed so far: a Weebly brochure site (statenislandusa.com) behind Cloudflare, with no CMS of record, no application, and no API.

Only two NYC Open Data assets carry the agency label 'Staten Island Borough President (BPSI)': 'BP Assist Helpline Requests' (3fes-huds), an aggregate count-by-request-type table that ends at FY20, and 'Category Master File' (mmut-uup9), a zero-column blob.

None of the borough president's actual NYC Charter functions — advisory ULURP land-use recommendations, community board appointments, discretionary capital/expense funding, and Borough Board resolutions — is published as data anywhere the office owns; they exist only as PDFs, HTML, or inside other agencies' systems (City Planning ZAP, the adopted budget Schedule C).

The office's two real transactions are un-contracted: BP Assist (report a pothole/litter/graffiti issue) is a web widget, and the Community Board Membership Application is an opaque Weebly form posting to weebly.com/apps/formSubmit.php.

Net-new write surface: apply to a community board (CommunityBoardApplication), plus a BP Assist constituent request. The strategic finding is that all five NYC borough-president offices are near-identical thin sites, making this a candidate for one shared, federated Borough President API keyed by borough — not five bespoke builds.

apis_observed.note: Only 2 BPSI assets: 3fes-huds (aggregate BP Assist counts, ends FY20) and mmut-uup9 (zero-column file). A SODA /resource/3fes-huds.json endpoint exists but the office does not consume it.
Where the Community Board Membership Application posts. Opaque _u… field names; not an API, not documented, not agent-usable.
Weebly built-in site search; no JSON contract.
Content only; no content API, no JSON, no OpenAPI.

fruit.name: Land-use / ULURP recommendations
fruit.notes: The BP's NYC Charter advisory review of every land-use application. Published only as PDFs/notices; the underlying applications live in City Planning's ZAP system, not here. No BPSI API or dataset.

fruit.name: Community board appointments
fruit.notes: The BP appoints all members of Staten Island's three community boards (CB1 North Shore, CB2 Mid-Island, CB3 South Shore), half on council-member nomination. No published roster dataset.

fruit.name: Discretionary funding awards
fruit.notes: Capital and expense allocations the BP makes to agencies and organizations. Requests accepted 'via mail or email' only; awards appear only inside the adopted citywide budget (Schedule C). No BPSI API or dataset.

fruit.name: Borough Board resolutions & reports
fruit.notes: The Staten Island Borough Board (BP + council members + community board chairs) meets monthly and issues resolutions/reports. Published only as monthly PDFs going back years. No dataset, no API.

fruit.name: Community events & concerts
fruit.notes: Borough community calendar and the 'Sounds of Summer' concert series. HTML/embedded only; no iCal feed, dataset, or API.

fruit.name: BP Assist help line
fruit.notes: The office's one transactional surface: report potholes, litter, graffiti, relayed to the help line and on to a city agency. The only related Open Data (3fes-huds) is AGGREGATE annual counts by request type and ends at FY20 — never the individual request.

fruit.name: Apply to a community board
fruit.notes: The Community Board Membership Application — the net-new WRITE surface. Today an opaque Weebly form posting to weebly.com/apps/formSubmit.php with _u… field names; no API, no structured intake, no Open Data twin.

fruit.name: Category Master File
fruit.notes: The only other BPSI asset — a 'file' type with zero exposed columns, effectively a lookup blob rather than a queryable dataset.
